events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes "excessive cold spring to force alignment" in spool erection & fit-up. Cold springing is the intentional cutting short of pipe to create a pre-stress when the system is at operating temperature. ASME B31.3 allows cold springing but has specific requirements - it should be done properly and not excessively to force misaligned components into place. Excessive cold spring to force alignment is a defect - it introduces unwanted stresses. This is non-compliant work.

Cold spring per ASME B31.3: Cold spring is permitted but the benefit is limited to 2/3 of the expansion stress range. However, using cold spring to force alignment of misaligned pipe is not acceptable - it indicates poor fit-up and introduces residual stresses not accounted for in design.

This is a fail. The condition is installed-defective and non-compliant. Risk is high because excessive cold spring can lead to stress corrosion cracking, premature failure, or joint failure.
